platform.name: Spring Boot
platform.category: Frameworks, mobile, games, hardware, and creative ecosystems

assumptions: Spring Boot's documented quickstart does not require a Spring, VMware, or cloud account.
No Spring Boot project exists yet.
JDK and IDE installations may still be required as external prerequisites.

developer_goal: Generate a Spring Boot web project, add the Hello World /hello endpoint, run it locally, and verify the browser response.

first_success.outcome: Embedded Tomcat serves the documented Hello World endpoint locally.

route_selection.rule: Prefer the vendor-labeled Super quick route over the more general Building an Application guide.
route_selection.selected: Spring Quickstart Guide Hello World endpoint

candidates.name: Spring Quickstart Guide
candidates.reason: The Spring Boot project page labels this Super quick and it ends at the /hello browser response.

candidates.name: Building an Application with Spring Boot
candidates.reason: Labeled more general and continues through tests, Actuator, and build artifacts.

candidates.name: Developing Your First Spring Boot Application (manual project files)
candidates.reason: Creates Maven or Gradle files manually and continues through an executable JAR.

required_path.action: Install or verify a compatible JDK and choose an IDE or editor that can open the generated project.

required_fields.label: JDK install or verification
required_fields.notes: Quickstart recommends BellSoft Liberica JDK 17 or 21.

required_fields.label: IDE or editor choice
required_fields.notes: Examples include IntelliJ IDEA, VS Code with Spring Boot Extension Pack, or Eclipse with Spring Tools.

required_path.observable_result: A JDK and IDE are available to open and run the generated project.

required_path.action: On start.spring.io, create a web project, add the Web dependency, and Generate the ZIP.

required_fields.label: Initializr project metadata and build settings
required_fields.notes: Public Initializr UI exposes Project, Language, Spring Boot version, Group, Artifact, Packaging, and Java fields; the quickstart text does not enumerate required values beyond a web project.

required_path.observable_result: A downloadable Spring Boot project ZIP is generated.

required_path.action: Download, unpack, and open the project, then add the documented /hello controller code to DemoApplication.java.
required_path.observable_result: The project contains the RestController /hello mapping.

required_path.action: From the project folder, run the generated Gradle wrapper bootRun command for the developer's OS.
required_path.observable_result: Spring Boot starts embedded Tomcat on localhost:8080.

prerequisites.description: A JDK; the quickstart recommends BellSoft Liberica JDK 17 or 21.
An IDE or editor capable of opening the generated project.
Browser access to start.spring.io and permission to download and unpack a ZIP.

unavoidable_waits.description: Gradle wrapper resolution and first bootRun startup before Tomcat listens on port 8080.

platform_outcomes.description: Spring Initializr generates the project archive with Spring Boot and the Web starter.
Embedded Tomcat serves /hello after bootRun succeeds.

excluded.item: Optional ?name= query-parameter experiment and pop quiz
excluded.reason: Presented after the Hello World response as enrichment, not the terminal check.

excluded.item: Building an Application guide extras (tests, Actuator, packaging)
excluded.reason: A longer peer route, not the selected Super quick path.

sources.title: Spring Boot project page
evidence_supported: Super quick versus more general guide labeling

sources.title: Spring Initializr
evidence_supported: Public Initializr form controls for project metadata and dependencies

uncertainties.question: How should the corpus account-creation starting boundary map for Spring Boot, which has no required vendor account on the quickstart?
uncertainties.impact: Prevents a verified account-creation-to-first-success measurement even though the local Hello World path is otherwise documented.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Corpus policy for open-source frameworks without platform accounts.

uncertainties.question: Which exact Spring Initializr field values are required beside adding Web?
uncertainties.impact: Build tool, language, package name, and Java version change generated files and run commands.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Official quickstart text that enumerates every required Initializr field, or a dated walkthrough that records the defaults used.

uncertainties.question: Which OS and IDE installation journey is in scope on a clean machine?
uncertainties.impact: External JDK and IDE setup can dominate the action count and is only linked, not fully specified, by the quickstart.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A bounded OS/IDE assumption plus evidence of which install steps are mandatory versus skippable.
